Title: xCatalog - Search Products Query
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: All tests MUST execute in GraphiQL UI via browser (playwright-edge). Do NOT use curl/API calls. Follow graphiql-interaction.md for CodeMirror interaction. GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; products in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" query: "laptop") { totalCount items { id name code imgSrc } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response from response panel
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] data.products object present in response
[DATA] totalCount > 0 for valid query term
[DATA] items[] non-empty; each item contains id, name, code
[DATA] imgSrc is a valid URL or null
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same results returned
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; totalCount = 0 for known query term; missing id/name/code on items; GraphiQL shows error banner

Title: xCatalog - Get Product Detail Query
Preconditions: Product ID known from GQL-001; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); productId from GQL-001 result; currencyCode="USD"
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { product(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" id: "<PRODUCT_ID>") { id name descriptions { content } prices { list { amount } sale { amount } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response from response panel
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] product object returned with correct id
[DATA] descriptions[] non-empty; content populated
[MATH] prices.list.amount > 0
[DATA] prices.sale present (may be null if no sale active)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute query → same product returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; product null; descriptions empty; prices missing list amount

[ACT] Type query: query { categories(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { id name slug childCategories { id name } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result

[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL

Title: xCatalog - Product Facets/Filters Query
Preconditions: Products with properties configured; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); facet includes brand/price range; filter combinations
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" facet: "brand") { totalCount items { id name } term_facets { name label terms { term label count isSelected } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note facet values
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type filtered query with term facet applied
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — compare filtered vs unfiltered results
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] term_facets[] non-empty; at minimum brand facet present
[DATA] Each facet value has label and count > 0
[DATA] Filtered result set is smaller than unfiltered set
[DATA] Combining two filters uses AND logic (result set narrows further)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Filter counts match product count in filtered response
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; term_facets[] empty when facet-enabled properties exist; filter does not narrow results; facet count mismatches

Title: xCatalog - Product Variations Query
Preconditions: Configurable products with variations (size, color) exist in catalog; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); parentProductId (configurable product with known variations)
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { product(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" id: "<PARENT_ID>") { id name variations { id name properties { name value } prices { list { amount } } availabilityData { availableQuantity } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify variation properties and pricing
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] variations[] non-empty
[DATA] Each variation has distinct property combination (e.g., size+color unique)
[DATA] Each variation has prices.list.amount > 0
[DATA] availabilityData differs per variation if stock differs (or all show available if not tracked)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute query → same variations returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; variations[] empty for known configurable product; all variations same price when they should differ; availabilityData not populated

Title: xCatalog - Product Listing Price During Reindex Lag Window
Preconditions: Product with known price; Elasticsearch indexing enabled; Admin API accessible; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: productCode=@td(PROD_DEFAULT.sku); storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); priceListId=@td(PRICELIST_USD.id); originalPrice=<record before change>; newPrice=<choose test value>; reindexWindowSeconds=60
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type products query for @td(PROD_DEFAULT.sku) → Execute → Read response → record as originalPrice
[ACT] Change @td(PROD_DEFAULT.sku) price via: PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices with newPrice
[ACT] Immediately clear editor → type same products query → Execute
[READ] Read response — document whether old or new price returned (lag acceptable per BL-CROSS-002)
[WAIT] Wait 60 seconds for reindex to complete
[ACT] Clear editor → type same products query → Execute
[READ] Read response — verify price now reflects newPrice
Assertions:
[DATA] Step 4 result: originalPrice MAY be returned (acceptable reindex lag of 30-60s)
[DATA] Step 7 result: prices.list.amount = newPrice (reindex must have completed)
[DATA] If step 7 still shows originalPrice after 90s total: flag as reindex failure
[DATA] Document actual observed lag duration
[ERRORS] No errors[] in any query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Multiple re-executions confirm price converges to newPrice
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: xCatalog still shows originalPrice after 90 seconds total; errors[] on xCatalog query; REST price update returns non-200; price shows as 0 after change
Cleanup: restore original price via Admin API

Title: xCatalog - Range Filter Syntax: Inclusive vs Exclusive Boundaries
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; products with known prices exist; auth not required for product queries
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" filter: "price.usd:[50 TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_Inclusive
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with exclusive bounds: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" filter: "price.usd:(50 TO 100)" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_Exclusive
Assertions:
[DATA] Inclusive [50 TO 100]: all items have price.actual.amount >= 50 AND <= 100
[DATA] Exclusive (50 TO 100): all items have price.actual.amount > 50 AND < 100
[MATH] Count_Inclusive >= Count_Exclusive (inclusive includes boundary products)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
[DATA] Products at exactly $50.00 or $100.00 appear in inclusive but not exclusive result
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute inclusive query → same totalCount
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Inclusive and exclusive returning identical counts; products at exact boundary appearing in exclusive result; errors[] in response; totalCount = 0 for valid price range

Title: xCatalog - Open-Ended Range Filter Syntax
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; products with varying prices exist
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with upper-bounded range: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" filter: "price.usd:(TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_UpTo100
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with lower-bounded range: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" filter: "price.usd:[100 TO)" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_From100
Assertions:
[DATA] (TO 100]: all items have price.actual.amount <= 100
[DATA] [100 TO): all items have price.actual.amount >= 100
[MATH] Count_UpTo100 + Count_From100 >= total products (products at exactly $100 counted in both)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
[DATA] No products with amount > 100 in the (TO 100] result
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute each query → same totalCount
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Products above $100 appearing in (TO 100] result; products below $100 in [100 TO) result; errors[] in response; empty result for ranges containing known products

Title: xCatalog - Range Filter on Non-Price Property (Underscore Field Name)
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; products with underscore-named property exist (e.g., Volume_ml in Alcoholic Drinks category)
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); currencyCode="USD"; propertyName=Volume_ml; categoryContext=alcoholic drinks (use a category with numeric underscore-named properties)
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query filtering by underscore property: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" filter: "Volume_ml:[200 TO 550]" facet: "Volume_ml" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name properties { name value } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — check totalCount and item properties
Assertions:
[DATA] totalCount > 0 for valid Volume_ml range (observed: 12 products)
[DATA] Returned items have Volume_ml property values within 200–550 range (observed: 200, 220, 330, 500, 550)
[DATA] No items with Volume_ml < 200 (e.g., 33ml products excluded)
[ERRORS] No errors[] related to filter logic (note: ARGUMENT_NULL errors may appear for items with null properties — this is a pre-existing data quality issue, not a filter bug)
[DATA] Filter parameter 'Volume_ml' is preserved in the request (not truncated to 'Volume')
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → consistent results
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: totalCount = 0 for range containing known products; items with Volume_ml=33 appearing in 200–550 range; field name truncated in filter parameter; errors[] indicating filter parsing failure

Title: xCatalog - Facet Parameter Returns Range Aggregation Bucket
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; category with price-range products exists
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with explicit range facet parameter: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" facet: "price.usd:[0 TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount range_facets { name ranges { from to count isSelected label } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read range_facets in response
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with different range bucket: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" facet: "price.usd:[100 TO 500]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount range_facets { name ranges { from to count isSelected label } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read range_facets in response — compare bucket count
Assertions:
[DATA] range_facets array contains entry with name 'price'
[DATA] ranges[] contains at least one bucket object with from, to, count, isSelected, label fields
[MATH] Bucket count is non-negative and <= totalCount
[DATA] isSelected reflects whether the range matches the facet parameter
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
[DATA] Second query returns a different bucket count for [100 TO 500] range
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute → same bucket counts
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: range_facets empty or null (note: facet:"price" alone returns empty range_facets — explicit range syntax required); bucket missing count field; errors[] in response; bucket count exceeds totalCount

Title: xCatalog - products Pagination Using first and after Cursor
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; catalog contains more than 10 products (totalCount > 10)
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type first-page query: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" first: 5 cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } items { id name } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read pageInfo.hasNextPage and pageInfo.endCursor — record endCursor as <CURSOR>
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type second-page query using cursor: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" first: 5 after: "<CURSOR>" cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } items { id name } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read items[] from second page — verify no overlap with first page
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Both responses show data without error banner
[DATA] Page 1: items[] length = 5; pageInfo.hasNextPage = true (assuming totalCount > 10)
[DATA] Page 1: pageInfo.endCursor is a non-empty string (opaque cursor token)
[DATA] Page 2: items[] length = 5 (or fewer on last page); none of the ids match page 1 ids
[DATA] Both pages: totalCount is the same value (pagination does not change total)
[MATH] Collecting all pages yields totalCount items with no duplicates
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute page 1 query → same items and cursor
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: pageInfo missing hasNextPage or endCursor fields; page 2 items overlap with page 1; totalCount differs between pages; errors[] present; cursor token rejected as invalid

Title: xCatalog - products Query with sort Parameter Orders Results
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; catalog contains multiple products with different names and prices
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query sorted by name ascending: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" sort: "name:asc" first: 10 cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { id name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Record the sequence of name values from items[] as Name_Asc
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query sorted by name descending: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" sort: "name:desc" first: 10 cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { id name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Record the sequence of name values from items[] as Name_Desc
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Both responses show data without error banner
[DATA] Name_Asc: items[] ordered alphabetically A → Z (case-insensitive comparison acceptable)
[DATA] Name_Desc: items[] ordered alphabetically Z → A — the reverse of Name_Asc
[DATA] Name_Asc first item equals Name_Desc last item (symmetric ordering)
[DATA] totalCount is identical in both responses
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute asc query → same order
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: items[] in same order regardless of sort parameter; sort parameter silently ignored; errors[] present; totalCount differs between sort variants

Title: xCatalog - products Filter by Category Subtree and Price Floor
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; categories exist with products; catalog root ID known
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[SETUP] Get a category ID to scope the subtree: query { categories(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US" first: 1) { items { id name } } } → save id as <CAT_ID>
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with category subtree + price floor filter (mirrors storefront base filter): query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US" currencyCode: "USD" filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id)/<CAT_ID> price.USD:(0 TO)" first: 5) { totalCount items { id name price { actual { amount currency { code } } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify products belong to category and have price > 0
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] totalCount > 0
[DATA] All items have price.actual.amount > 0 (price floor filter applied)
[DATA] items[] contain products from the specified category subtree
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
[MATH] No item has price.actual.amount = 0 (filtered out by price.USD:(0 TO))
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same totalCount and items
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: totalCount = 0 for category with known products; items with price = 0 appearing despite price floor filter; errors[] in response; products from other categories appearing in filtered results

Title: xCatalog - products Filter by inStock_variations (In-Stock vs Out-of-Stock)
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; catalog contains both in-stock and out-of-stock products
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with inStock_variations:true filter: query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US" currencyCode: "USD" filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) inStock_variations:true price.USD:(0 TO)" first: 5) { totalCount items { id name availabilityData { isInStock availableQuantity } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — record totalCount as <IN_STOCK_COUNT> and verify availabilityData
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query WITHOUT inStock_variations filter (reset — base filter only): query { products(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US" currencyCode: "USD" filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) price.USD:(0 TO)" first: 5) { totalCount items { id name availabilityData { isInStock availableQuantity } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — record totalCount as <ALL_COUNT>
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Both responses show data without error banner
[DATA] inStock_variations:true → all returned items have availabilityData.isInStock = true (or at least one variation in stock)
[DATA] inStock_variations:true → <IN_STOCK_COUNT> < <ALL_COUNT> (filter excludes out-of-stock products)
[DATA] Base filter (no inStock_variations) → returns ALL products regardless of stock status — includes both in-stock and out-of-stock
[MATH] <ALL_COUNT> > <IN_STOCK_COUNT> (removing inStock_variations filter widens the result set)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:

Title: xCatalog - brands Query — List All Brands (Storefront Pattern)
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; brands exist in catalog; store configured
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); cultureName="en-US"; currencyCode="USD"; first=1000 (storefront loads all brands at once)
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query matching storefront GetBrands pattern: query { brands(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" cultureName: "en-US" currencyCode: "USD" first: 1000 sort: "name:asc") { totalCount items { id name bannerUrl logoUrl description featured permalink brandPropertyValue } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — record totalCount as <TOTAL_BRANDS> and first brand name as <BRAND_NAME>
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] totalCount > 0 (brands exist in catalog)
[DATA] items[] sorted alphabetically A → Z by name (sort: "name:asc")
[DATA] Each brand has id (non-empty string — brand name used as ID) and name (non-empty string)
[DATA] Each brand has permalink (non-null, format: "brands/<slug>")
[DATA] featured is boolean (true for TOP BRANDS section brands)
[DATA] brandPropertyValue is present (raw catalog property value)
[DATA] logoUrl and bannerUrl are strings (may be null — most brands lack images)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same totalCount and brand order
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: totalCount = 0 when brands are configured; items[] empty; brand missing id or name; items not sorted despite sort param; permalink null; errors[] in response
Cleanup: NOTE: Storefront uses first:1000 and performs client-side A-Z filtering and keyword search — no server-side keyword param is used

Title: xCatalog - ProductType Full-Field Schema Coverage
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: Execute in GraphiQL UI ({{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql) via playwright browser. Product ID known (use @td(PROD_DEFAULT.sku) or get from GQL-001). No auth required for catalog queries.
Test_Data: storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); productId from GQL-001 or @td(PROD_DEFAULT.sku); currencyCode="USD"; cultureName="en-US"
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { product(storeId: "@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)" id: "<PRODUCT_ID>" currencyCode: "USD" cultureName: "en-US") { id code catalogId productType minQuantity maxQuantity packSize slug name imgSrc outerId gtin manufacturerPartNumber weightUnit weight measureUnit height width length brandName hasVariations inWishlist isPurchased seoInfo { id name semanticUrl isActive } descriptions { id content languageCode reviewType } category { id name slug } brand { id name } availabilityData { availableQuantity isBuyable isAvailable isInStock isActive isTrackInventory } images { id url name sortOrder } price { list { amount formattedAmount currency { code } } listWithTax { amount } sale { amount } saleWithTax { amount } actual { amount formattedAmount } actualWithTax { amount } discountAmount { amount } discountPercent currency { code symbol } tierPrices { quantity price { amount } } pricelistId } prices { list { amount } sale { amount } minQuantity pricelistId } minVariationPrice { list { amount } actual { amount } } properties { id name type valueType values { value valueId } } keyProperties { id name values { value } } assets { id url name size typeId group } breadcrumbs { title seoPath typeName } vendor { id name rating { value reviewCount } } rating { value reviewCount } videos { name thumbnailUrl videoUrl sortOrder } variations { id name code availabilityData { isBuyable isAvailable isInStock availableQuantity } price { list { amount } actual { amount } } images { url } properties { name values { value } } } associations { items { associatedObjectId associatedObjectType quantity tags } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read full response — inspect every top-level and nested field
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response — query must not fail due to unresolvable fields
[DATA] id, code, name, slug — all non-null strings
[DATA] catalogId — non-null string
[DATA] productType — non-null string (Physical, Digital, etc.)
[DATA] imgSrc — string or null (not an error object)
[DATA] seoInfo — object with semanticUrl string
[DATA] descriptions[] — array (may be empty but not null)
[DATA] category — object with id, name, slug (all non-null for assigned products)
[DATA] availabilityData — object; isBuyable, isAvailable, isInStock are booleans (not null)
[DATA] images[] — array of objects; each has url (non-null string)
[DATA] price — object; list.amount is number > 0; actual.amount is number > 0; currency.code is string
[DATA] price.formattedAmount — non-empty string (e.g. "$123.45")
[DATA] properties[] — array; each has name (string) and values[] (array)
[DATA] breadcrumbs[] — array; each has title and seoPath
[DATA] variations[] — array (may be empty for non-variable products; should not be null)
[DATA] rating — object with value (number) and reviewCount (number)
[DATA] No field returns an error object or __typename mismatch instead of data
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute identical query → response structure identical (no flaky resolvers)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Any top-level field returns null unexpectedly (regression); errors[] non-empty; nested MoneyType missing amount field; availabilityData booleans are null instead of true/false; price.list.amount is 0 for a priced product; images[].url is null; GraphiQL error banner
